16	// TestInstall_InTreeWritesProjectTreeAndLinks verifies AC1:
17	// With an in-tree <project>/.ddx/, ddx install <name> (no --global) writes to
18	// <project>/.ddx/plugins/<name>/ and creates project-tier links under
19	// <project>/.claude/skills/<name> and <project>/.agents/skills/<name>
20	// resolving into that plugins/ dir.
21	func TestInstall_InTreeWritesProjectTreeAndLinks(t *testing.T) {
22		workDir := t.TempDir()
23		homeDir := t.TempDir()
24		t.Setenv("HOME", homeDir)
25	
26		// Force in-tree mode by pre-creating .ddx/.
27		require.NoError(t, os.MkdirAll(filepath.Join(workDir, ddxroot.DirName), 0o755))
28	
29		localPlugin := t.TempDir()
30		makeLocalPlugin(t, localPlugin, "myplugin")
31	
32		factory := NewCommandFactory(workDir)
33		output, err := executeCommand(factory.NewRootCommand(), "install", "myplugin", "--local", localPlugin, "--force")
34		require.NoError(t, err, output)
35	
36		// Plugin must land in the project in-tree location.
37		projectPluginDir := filepath.Join(workDir, ddxroot.DirName, "plugins", "myplugin")
38		pluginInfo, pluginStatErr := os.Lstat(projectPluginDir)
39		require.NoError(t, pluginStatErr, "project plugin dir must exist at %s", projectPluginDir)
40		assert.True(t, pluginInfo.Mode()&os.ModeSymlink != 0, "project plugin dir must be a symlink to the local checkout")
41	
42		// Project-tier skill links must be in the project directory and resolve into the plugin source.
43		for _, surface := range []string{".agents/skills", ".claude/skills"} {
44			skillLink := filepath.Join(workDir, surface, "myplugin-skill")
45			skillInfo, skillErr := os.Lstat(skillLink)
46			require.NoError(t, skillErr, "%s/myplugin-skill must exist for in-tree install", surface)
47			assert.True(t, skillInfo.Mode()&os.ModeSymlink != 0 || skillInfo.IsDir(),
48				"%s/myplugin-skill must be a symlink or dir for in-tree install", surface)
49	
50			// Skill link must resolve into the same source tree as .ddx/plugins/myplugin.
51			target, readErr := os.Readlink(skillLink)
52			require.NoError(t, readErr, "%s must be a symlink", skillLink)
53			if !filepath.IsAbs(target) {
54				target = filepath.Join(filepath.Dir(skillLink), target)
55			}
56			target, _ = filepath.Abs(target)
57			assert.True(t, strings.HasPrefix(target, localPlugin),
58				"%s must resolve into the plugin source dir; got %s", skillLink, target)
59		}
60	
61		// Nothing must land in the home directory's skill dirs.
62		_, noAgentErr := os.Lstat(filepath.Join(homeDir, ".agents", "skills", "myplugin-skill"))
63		assert.True(t, os.IsNotExist(noAgentErr), "home .agents/skills must not be created for in-tree install")
64		_, noClaudeErr := os.Lstat(filepath.Join(homeDir, ".claude", "skills", "myplugin-skill"))
65		assert.True(t, os.IsNotExist(noClaudeErr), "home .claude/skills must not be created for in-tree install")
66	}

68	// TestInstall_ConventionModeWritesXDGProjectTree verifies AC2:
69	// With no <project>/.ddx/, ddx install <name> (no --global) writes to
70	// ${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/projects/<host>/<owner>/<repo>/plugins/<name>/ and the
71	// project agent-tier links point into that XDG plugins/ path.
72	// A git repo with a deterministic remote URL is used so host/owner/repo are stable.
73	func TestInstall_ConventionModeWritesXDGProjectTree(t *testing.T) {
74		workDir := t.TempDir()
75		homeDir := t.TempDir()
76		xdgDataHome := t.TempDir()
77		t.Setenv("HOME", homeDir)
78		t.Setenv("XDG_DATA_HOME", xdgDataHome)
79	
80		// Set up a git repo with a known remote so project identity is deterministic.
81		cleanEnv := gitpkg.CleanEnv()
82		for _, tc := range []struct {
83			args []string
84			dir  string
85		}{
86			{[]string{"git", "init"}, workDir},
87			{[]string{"git", "config", "user.email", "test@example.com"}, workDir},
88			{[]string{"git", "config", "user.name", "Test"}, workDir},
89			{[]string{"git", "remote", "add", "origin", "https://github.com/testowner/testrepo.git"}, workDir},
90		} {
91			cmd := exec.Command(tc.args[0], tc.args[1:]...)
92			cmd.Dir = tc.dir
93			cmd.Env = cleanEnv
94			require.NoError(t, cmd.Run(), "setup: %v", tc.args)
95		}
96	
97		// No .ddx/ in workDir — convention mode.
98		localPlugin := t.TempDir()
99		makeLocalPlugin(t, localPlugin, "myplugin")
100	
101		factory := NewCommandFactory(workDir)
102		output, err := executeCommand(factory.NewRootCommand(), "install", "myplugin", "--local", localPlugin, "--force")
103		require.NoError(t, err, output)
104	
105		// Convention root must be at the XDG projects path derived from the remote URL.
106		// The origin https://github.com/testowner/testrepo.git parses to github.com/testowner/testrepo.
107		expectedConventionRoot := filepath.Join(xdgDataHome, "ddx", "projects", "github.com", "testowner", "testrepo")
108		conventionPluginDir := filepath.Join(expectedConventionRoot, "plugins", "myplugin")
109		pluginInfo, pluginStatErr := os.Lstat(conventionPluginDir)

111		assert.True(t, pluginInfo.Mode()&os.ModeSymlink != 0, "convention plugin dir must be a symlink to the local checkout")
112	
113		// Project agent-tier links must be in the project directory (not in the convention root).
114		for _, surface := range []string{".agents/skills", ".claude/skills"} {
115			skillLink := filepath.Join(workDir, surface, "myplugin-skill")
116			skillInfo, skillErr := os.Lstat(skillLink)
117			require.NoError(t, skillErr, "%s/myplugin-skill must exist for convention install", surface)
118			assert.True(t, skillInfo.Mode()&os.ModeSymlink != 0 || skillInfo.IsDir(),
119				"%s/myplugin-skill must be a symlink or dir for convention install", surface)
120	
121			// Skill link must resolve into the local plugin source.
122			target, readErr := os.Readlink(skillLink)
123			require.NoError(t, readErr, "%s must be a symlink", skillLink)
124			if !filepath.IsAbs(target) {
125				target = filepath.Join(filepath.Dir(skillLink), target)
126			}
127			target, _ = filepath.Abs(target)
128			assert.True(t, strings.HasPrefix(target, localPlugin),
129				"%s must resolve into the plugin source dir; got %s", skillLink, target)
130		}
131	
132		// Home directory must not be polluted.
133		_, noAgentErr := os.Lstat(filepath.Join(homeDir, ".agents", "skills", "myplugin-skill"))
134		assert.True(t, os.IsNotExist(noAgentErr), "home .agents/skills must not be created for convention install")
135		_, noClaudeErr := os.Lstat(filepath.Join(homeDir, ".claude", "skills", "myplugin-skill"))
136		assert.True(t, os.IsNotExist(noClaudeErr), "home .claude/skills must not be created for convention install")

16	// commitTestFiles stages and commits the listed project-relative paths.
17	func commitTestFiles(t *testing.T, dir string, relPaths ...string) {
18		t.Helper()
19		cleanEnv := gitpkg.CleanEnv()
20		for _, rel := range relPaths {
21			add := exec.Command("git", "add", rel)
22			add.Dir = dir
23			add.Env = cleanEnv
24			require.NoError(t, add.Run(), "git add %s", rel)
25		}
26		commit := exec.Command("git", "commit", "--allow-empty", "-m", "test: commit update target files")
27		commit.Dir = dir
28		commit.Env = cleanEnv
29		require.NoError(t, commit.Run(), "git commit")
30	}

32	// writeRegistryInstalled writes a fake installed.yaml under ~HOME/.ddx/ so that
33	// performUpdate sees the listed files as registry-installed update targets.
34	func writeRegistryInstalled(t *testing.T, entries []registry.InstalledEntry) {
35		t.Helper()
36		state := &registry.InstalledState{Installed: entries}
37		require.NoError(t, registry.SaveState(state))
38	}
39	
40	// TestUpdate_ForceReplacesStaleSymlinks verifies that ddx update --force
41	// replaces legacy symlinks under .agents/skills/ with real files.
42	// This covers the FEAT-015 requirement that skills.Install is always invoked
43	// with Force:true during update (via refreshShippedSkills).
44	func TestUpdate_ForceReplacesStaleSymlinks(t *testing.T) {
45		homeDir := t.TempDir()
46		t.Setenv("HOME", homeDir)
47	
48		te := NewTestEnvironment(t, WithGitInit(false))
49	
50		// Create .agents/skills/ddx as a symlink (pre-migration state).
51		agentSkillsDir := filepath.Join(te.Dir, ".agents", "skills")
52		require.NoError(t, os.MkdirAll(agentSkillsDir, 0o755))
53	
54		fakeTarget := filepath.Join(t.TempDir(), "old-global-ddx")
55		require.NoError(t, os.MkdirAll(fakeTarget, 0o755))
56		symlinkPath := filepath.Join(agentSkillsDir, "ddx")
57		require.NoError(t, os.Symlink(fakeTarget, symlinkPath))
58	
59		// Verify the symlink exists before update.
60		info, err := os.Lstat(symlinkPath)
61		require.NoError(t, err)
62		require.True(t, info.Mode()&os.ModeSymlink != 0, "expected symlink before update")
63	
64		// Run ddx update --force; this should call refreshShippedSkills which
65		// calls skills.Install with Force:true, replacing the symlink.
66		_, err = te.RunCommand("update", "--force")
67		// The command may fail due to network operations (checking plugin updates),
68		// but refreshShippedSkills runs unconditionally before any network call.
69		// We tolerate network errors but require the symlink to be gone.
70		_ = err
71	
72		// After update, the symlink must be replaced with a real directory.
73		info, err = os.Lstat(symlinkPath)
74		if os.IsNotExist(err) {
75			// Skill was not installed (embedded FS may not have ddx skill in test);
76			// acceptable — the symlink is gone either way.
77			return
78		}
79		require.NoError(t, err)
80		if info.Mode()&os.ModeSymlink != 0 {
81			t.Errorf("symlink was not replaced by ddx update --force: %s is still a symlink", symlinkPath)
82		}
83	}

161	// TestUpdate_DiscardLocalOverwritesDirty asserts that ddx update --force
162	// --discard-local succeeds when an installed file is dirty, and that the
163	// original content is saved to .ddx/update-backup/<timestamp>/<path> [AC3].
164	func TestUpdate_DiscardLocalOverwritesDirty(t *testing.T) {
165		homeDir := t.TempDir()
166		t.Setenv("HOME", homeDir)
167	
168		te := NewTestEnvironment(t) // git-initialised by default
169	
170		installedFile := filepath.Join("library", "skills", "ddx", "SKILL.md")
171		writeRegistryInstalled(t, []registry.InstalledEntry{{
172			Name:    "ddx",
173			Version: "0.4.7",
174			Type:    registry.PackageTypePlugin,
175			Files:   []string{installedFile},
176		}})
177	
178		te.CreateFile(installedFile, "original committed content\n")
179		commitTestFiles(t, te.Dir, installedFile)
180	
181		// Make dirty — this is the content that must be saved in the backup.
182		dirtyContent := "modified content — not yet committed\n"
183		te.CreateFile(installedFile, dirtyContent)
184	
185		// Run with --discard-local; tolerate network errors from InstallPackage.
186		output, err := te.RunCommand("update", "--force", "--discard-local")
187		_ = err
188	
189		// Must NOT produce the dirty-file error.
190		assert.NotContains(t, output, "--force --discard-local", /*as error msg*/
191			"--discard-local should suppress the dirty-file block; got: %q", output)
192	
193		// Backup directory must exist under .ddx/update-backup/<timestamp>/
194		backupRoot := filepath.Join(te.Dir, ddxroot.DirName, "update-backup")
195		entries, readErr := os.ReadDir(backupRoot)
196		require.NoError(t, readErr, "update-backup directory should be created")
197		require.NotEmpty(t, entries, "at least one timestamped backup dir should exist")
198	
199		// The backup file must contain the dirty (pre-overwrite) content so the
200		// operator can recover their uncommitted changes.
201		backupFile := filepath.Join(backupRoot, entries[0].Name(), installedFile)
202		data, readErr := os.ReadFile(backupFile)
203		require.NoError(t, readErr, "backup file should be readable: %s", backupFile)
204		assert.Equal(t, dirtyContent, string(data),
205			"backup should contain the dirty (pre-overwrite) content")
206	}

314	func TestUpdate_PartialDirtyBatchHaltsCleanly(t *testing.T) {
315		homeDir := t.TempDir()

325			Type:    registry.PackageTypePlugin,
326			Files:   []string{dirtyFile, cleanFile},
327		}})

337		require.Error(t, err, "update should fail when any target file is dirty")
